_meta.note: ANONYMIZED, English, k>=3-org distilled patterns from Binclusive's dynamic-audit corpus. Failure-shapes clustered offline by LLM (frozen in data/clusters/), gated k>=3-distinct-org and anonymized by deterministic code. Contains NO customer identifiers (org/project ids, elements, urls stripped; frequency as tier only). Regenerate by re-running the distiller.

patterns.failureShape: An informative image has alt text that is a filename, technical id, or generic placeholder ("image.jpg", "pim_bu_100093", "Slider Image 4", "icon", "cover"), so screen-reader users get a meaningless name instead of the image's content.
patterns.fix: Write alt text that conveys the image's meaning or purpose. Never use filenames, ids, or generic placeholders. If the image is purely decorative, use an empty alt (alt="") instead.

patterns.failureShape: An image's alt text is excessively long or redundant — a paragraph of text, or a concatenation that duplicates adjacent visible text — so screen-reader users hear bloated, repeated content.
patterns.fix: Keep alt text concise (typically under ~125 characters) and non-redundant. If the same information is already in adjacent text, mark the image decorative with alt="".

patterns.failureShape: An image has alt text that is present but wrong or insufficient — outdated, mismatched to the destination, or too vague to describe a complex visual — so the announced text misleads or under-informs screen-reader users.
patterns.fix: Make the alt text accurately and sufficiently describe the image's content and purpose in context. Update stale alt text and expand vague names so they match what the image actually conveys.

patterns.failureShape: A complex image (chart, diagram, SVG/div-based graphic, or gallery) has no text alternative or position information, so the data or sequence it conveys is lost to screen-reader users.
patterns.fix: Give complex images a text alternative that conveys their data or purpose (e.g. a description or adjacent data table for charts). For galleries, expose position with aria-setsize/aria-posinset or descriptive alt per item.

patterns.failureShape: Decorative icons — icon-font glyphs, chevrons, or raw unicode characters — are announced by the screen reader as character codes or symbols, adding noise with no information.
patterns.fix: Hide decorative icons from assistive tech with aria-hidden="true". For icons that carry meaning, provide a text alternative (aria-label or an SVG <title>) instead.

patterns.failureShape: An image has no alt attribute at all (or is a background image standing in for content), often inside an image-only link, leaving the element with an empty accessible name.
patterns.fix: Add an alt attribute to every : descriptive text for informative images, or alt="" for decorative ones. For image-only links, the alt text must name the link destination.
